title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Group Insurance Commission ends GLP-1 coverage for state employees to cut expenditures despite concerns on health disparities.
Decision involves 22,000 members using GLP-1 drugs, costing the state $46 million; vote was 10-7 in favor of cut.
Governor Maura Healey pushed for $100 million savings, opting to remove GLP-1 coverage over other cost-cutting measures.

title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey and Attorney General Andrea Campbell reminded schools ICE needs judicial warrants to enter dorms.
Massachusetts officials issued guidance after ICE reportedly accessed Columbia University's building under false pretenses.
The issued guidance advises schools on designating private areas and posting signs to restrict unauthorized access.

title: Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A lawsuit filed in Boston challenges the National Park Service's content removal following a Trump administration order.
The executive order mandates removing content that 'disparages' Americans, affecting sites like Lowell National Historical Park.
Massachusetts Sen. Ed Markey criticizes the content removal, calling it an act of historical censorship and advocating restoration.

title: Mass. police dispatcher injured in Iran conflict
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Sudbury police dispatcher in the Navy Reserve suffered minor injuries during missile strikes near Dubai.
The Sudbury Police Department withheld the dispatcher's name for security and clarity reasons.
U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran killed Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei, sparking military escalations condemned by some lawmakers.

title: What Baylor Scheierman said about his fractured thumb
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Baylor Scheierman sustained a thumb fracture in a collision with Nets' Egor Demin on Friday night.
Scheierman played 32 minutes with a splint, scoring 12 points in the Celtics' win against the 76ers on Sunday.
He previously broke the same thumb in high school and plans to continue playing while managing the injury.

title: ‘It feels like home’: Chinatown celebrates the Year of the Horse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chinatown celebrated the Year of the Horse with a lively Lunar New Year parade featuring about 10 lion dance troupes.
The event, organized by Chinatown Main Street and the CCBA, included performances at local businesses as firecrackers exploded.
Gund Kwok, an all-women Asian dance troupe, performed with a horse costume, symbolizing strength and creativity.

title: Boston 25 News promotes Shiri Spear to chief meteorologist
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Shiri Spear has been promoted to chief meteorologist at Boston 25 News, overseeing weather coverage and leading the weather team.
Spear succeeds Kevin Lemanowicz, who stepped down after 30 years, including serving as chief meteorologist since the station's founding.
Spear holds a master's in atmospheric science and is a certified meteorologist, continuing to anchor Boston 25 Morning News.

title: R.I. prosecutor who told police ‘I’m an AG’ during arrest is back at work
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Devon Flanagan, a Rhode Island prosecutor, returned to work after pleading no contest to trespassing in Newport last August.
Flanagan was caught on video invoking her title during her arrest and has been reassigned to Providence County Superior Court.
Rhode Island Attorney General Peter Neronha criticized her actions, which led to her unpaid leave and reassignment.

title: Should pajamas be banned at Logan Airport? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: A poll on Boston.com shows 48% support banning pajamas at Logan Airport, while 42% oppose it.
The US Department of Transportation's campaign promotes 'civility' and questions if travelers are dressing with respect.
Boston.com readers are divided, citing comfort and respect in public spaces as contrasting viewpoints on pajama wear.

title: ‘A crisis of Trump’s creation’: Mass. politicians respond to strikes on Iran
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts politicians condemn Trump's strikes on Iran, calling it an illegal war without congressional authorization.
Senators Warren and Markey urge congressional action to limit Trump's military operations and promote diplomatic solutions.
Reps. Moulton, Clark, and Auchincloss criticize the strikes for lacking strategy, highlighting domestic concerns and legislative roles.
